filter_pushdown.note: applied at block level via zone map — skips ~86% of 600 blocks; lineitem is sorted by l_shipdate so this is maximally effective
applied row-level within qualifying blocks; most selective per-row predicate (3/11 distinct values); discount zone map provides negligible pruning since discount is unsorted
applied row-level within qualifying blocks; quantity zone map provides ~0% pruning; evaluate last in predicate chain

predicate_evaluation_order: l_shipdate (zone-map block skip first, then row-level check within block)
l_discount (most selective per-row; check second to short-circuit quickly)

data_structures.note: no grouping — single scalar SUM; each thread holds one double partial_sum; main thread reduces with std::accumulate after join

parallelism.note: divide the ~84 qualifying blocks (identified by shipdate zone map) among 64 threads; each thread accumulates its own double partial_sum; no locking needed during scan; final reduction is a sum of 64 doubles

index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks=600, then per block: [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_nrows] (12 bytes each); total 7204 bytes
index_usage.note: fits entirely in L1 cache (7204 bytes); mmap once before parallel phase; zero amortized overhead

index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks=600, then per block: [double min, double max, uint32_t block_nrows] (20 bytes each)
index_usage.expected_pruning: minimal — discount unsorted, most blocks span [0.00, 0.10]; negligible savings
index_usage.note: apply as opportunistic check after shipdate zone-map filtering; do not rely on this for significant pruning

index_usage.purpose: skipped — quantity unsorted, block_min always 1.0 so block_min >= 24.0 never true; provides ~0% pruning; not worth loading

scan_details.phase_1_block_selection: iterate shipdate zone map (600 entries); collect row_offsets of ~84 qualifying blocks; also check discount zone map per block as cheap secondary filter
scan_details.phase_2_column_loading: for each qualifying block, load 4 columns: l_shipdate (int32), l_discount (double), l_quantity (double), l_extendedprice (double); all read sequentially within block
scan_details.phase_3_row_filtering: fused predicate loop over block rows — check shipdate, then discount, then quantity; on pass: partial_sum += l_extendedprice[i] * l_discount[i]
scan_details.simd_strategy: AVX2 — process 4 doubles per instruction for discount/quantity/extprice; for shipdate use 8x int32 per AVX2 register; AND predicate masks together; conditionally accumulate using masked blend or gather

memory_details.data_volume_after_pruning: ~84 blocks × 100K rows × (4+8+8+8) bytes = ~235 MB across 4 columns
memory_details.access_pattern: sequential within each block; blocks are contiguous on disk since lineitem is sorted by shipdate — OS prefetcher effective
memory_details.l3_cache_behavior: each block (100K rows × 28 bytes ≈ 2.8 MB) slightly exceeds per-thread L3 slice (88MB/64=1.375MB) but sequential prefetch hides latency

estimated_cost.note: With 64 threads and 86% block skip, effective scan is ~235MB across 4 columns. HDD sequential throughput is the primary constraint; targeting sub-20ms competitive with DuckDB (20ms) and Umbra (14ms). Current GenDB baseline is 93ms — primary gain comes from correct zone-map usage skipping 86% of blocks.
